what: Monitor Large Trees using Sentinel-2 Time Series and Break Point Detection
title: Monitor Large Trees using Sentinel-2 Time Series and Break Point Detection
text: Large Tree Monitoring] (LTM) R-based project designed monitor large trees using Sentinel-2 satellite imagery Google Earth Engine (GEE). project offers tools data acquisition, preprocessing, analysis, visualization facilitate effective monitoring large tree dynamics time. Data Acquisition: Utilizes GEE rgee access download Sentinel-2 time series data specified regions time frames. requires users GEE account necessary rgee stack properly installed. Spectral Indices Calculation: Computes various spectral indices assess vegetation status, health characteristics. Data Preprocessing: Implements techniques handling missing data, regularization/interpolation, smoothing prepare time series analysis. Break Detection Analysis: Detects significant changes ‘breaks’ followed decreases vegetation greenness/amount time series data using established R packages. Visualization: Provides functions visualize data analysis results, aiding interpretation decision-making.

title: Monitor Large Trees using Sentinel-2 Time Series and Break Point Detection
text: Dependencies LTM leverages several R packages, including: rgee: Interfaces R Google Earth Engine. sf: Manages analyzes spatial data. terra: Handles raster data. tidyverse: collection packages data manipulation visualization. rgeeExtra: Extends rgee functionalities. imputeTS: Imputes missing time series data. bfast: Detects characterizes abrupt changes within time series. forecast: Provides methods tools time series forecasting. lubridate: Simplifies date time manipulation. ggplot2: Creates data visualizations.

title: Monitor Large Trees using Sentinel-2 Time Series and Break Point Detection
text: Google Earth Engine authentication: Authenticate GEE account using LTM_gee_auth.R script. Data retrieval: Use LTM_gee_data.R specify parameters download Sentinel-2 data. Preprocessing: Apply LTM_pre_proc.R clean prepare data. Break detection: Execute LTM_break_detection.R identify significant changes time series. Visualization: Utilize LTM_plots.R generate informative plots data analysis results. Shiny Application LTM includes Shiny application (LTM_shiny_app.R) provides interactive interface users visualize analyze data without extensive coding.

title: LargeTreeMonitoring package — LargeTreeMonitoring-package
text: LargeTreeMonitoring supports tree-level monitoring Sentinel-2 time series extracted Google Earth Engine. provides tools retrieve spectral-index observations individual tree locations, preprocess resulting time series, run multiple breakpoint-detection algorithms, validate detected breaks, summarize results, launch interactive Shiny workflow. Although package provides general tools time-series analysis breakpoint detection, current primary focus detection large tree loss removal.

title: LargeTreeMonitoring package — LargeTreeMonitoring-package
text: package organized around spidf object, data-frame subclass stores spectral-index observations request metadata. typical analysis starts initializing Google Earth Engine ltm_start_gee(), retrieving Sentinel-2 time series ltm_s2_get_data_point(), applying optional preprocessing, running one breakpoint-detection wrappers, collecting results ltm_ts_breaks() container.

title: LargeTreeMonitoring package — LargeTreeMonitoring-package
text: Initialize Google Earth Engine ltm_start_gee() verify stored session status ltm_check_gee_status(). Retrieve spidf time series ltm_s2_get_data_point(). Supported spectral indices listed SPECTRAL_INDICES_LIST, supported Sentinel-2 processing levels listed PROC_LEVELS_LIST. Optionally regularize smooth time series ltm_regularize_spidf(), ltm_apply_moving_quantile(), ltm_apply_whitaker(). Run breakpoint-detection wrappers : ltm_ed_detect_breaks() – Energy-divisive (ecp::e.divisive), ltm_cpm_detect_breaks() – Change Point Models (cpm::cpm-package), ltm_bfast01_detect_breaks() – BFAST (bfast::bfast-package), ltm_mcp_detect_breaks() – model based specific implementation ob Bayesian Regression Multiple Change Points (mcp::mcp-package), ltm_strucchange_detect_breaks() – Structural Change Linear Regression Models (strucchangeRcpp::breakpoints ), ltm_wbs_detect_breaks() – Wild Binary Segmentation multiple change-point detection (wbs::wbs-package). Store, inspect, flatten, plot, summarize results ltm_ts_breaks(), ltm_add_runs(), .data.frame.ts_breaks(), ltm_plot_spidf_ts(), plot_valid_breaks(), ltm_summarize_break_df().

title: Detect breakpoints using Bayesian change-point models — ltm_mcp_detect_breaks
text: wrapper uses internally defined priors based empirical quantiles standard deviation analysis series. Extra arguments ... passed mcp::mcp() resolving deprecated LargeTreeMonitoring aliases tresh_int thresh_change. Fits Bayesian one-change-point step model preprocessed analysis series using mcp::mcp(). analysis series obtained optional NA interpolation followed optional STL-based seasonal adjustment. fitted mcp model list(ysa ~ 1, ~ 1) par_x = "di", implying two constant-mean segments separated one estimated changepoint. Priors data-dependent directional, favoring decrease first segment level second. Let \(y_i\) denote raw input series ordered observations \(= 1, \ldots, n\). Missing values deterministically interpolated model fitting; denote interpolated series \(\tilde{y}_i\). seasonal adjustment enabled feasible, fitted series \(z_i = SA(\tilde{y}_i)\), \(SA(\cdot)\) denotes STL-based seasonal adjustment. Otherwise, \(z_i = \tilde{y}_i\). MCP specification list(ysa ~ 1, ~ 1) par_x = "di" defines one-change-point Gaussian step model \(z_i\): $$ z_i \mid c, \alpha_1, \alpha_2, \sigma \sim \mathrm{Normal}(\mu_i, \sigma^2) $$ $$ \mu_i = \alpha_1 (< c) + \alpha_2 (\ge c), \qquad = 1, \ldots, n $$ \(c = cp_1\) break index, \(\alpha_1 = int_1\) pre-break level, \(\alpha_2 = int_2\) post-break level. wrapper uses empirical, data-dependent priors: $$ \alpha_1 \sim \mathrm{Normal}(q_{0.75}, s^2)\ \text{truncated }\ [0, 1] $$ $$ \alpha_2 \sim \mathrm{Normal}(q_{0.25}, s^2)\ \text{truncated }\ [0, \alpha_1] $$ $$ c \sim \mathrm{Uniform}(1, n) $$ \(q_{0.75}\) \(q_{0.25}\) empirical 75th 25th percentiles analysis series \(s\) empirical standard deviation. prior specification encodes directed alternative post-break level exceed pre-break level. Accordingly, model designed detect downward level shifts rather arbitrary changes mean level.
